title: phacelia affinis var. typica
intro: Phacelia affinis var. typica, also known as the Desert Lavender Phacelia, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and wounds. The health benefits of Phacelia affinis var. typica include its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Phacelia affinis var. typica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used topically as a poultice or salve for skin conditions and wounds.
